fields.name: soon to be an initial task
Size Class
fields.definition:
Tells lightmapper desired res for structure bitmaps.
Numbers in parens are final sizes after compression

fields.name: structure vertex sink#this is the most that we can sink a soft surface link snow in the structure_bsp via vertex painting.
default instance fade start pixels#>end, pixel size we start stippling out. code default 36
default instance fade end pixels#<start, pixel size where we no longer render. code default 30
Clones
fields.definition:
Describes which other bsps are physical 'clones' of this bsp
This is used to determine how to attach 'position-only' elements, like decorators, to the bsps:
Each clone gets a separate copy of decorators that are in both.
Non-cloned bsps cannot split decorators this way - the decorator will be given to the lowest numbered bsp

fields.name: CONTROLS~
fields.definition: Ctrl-N: Creates a new area and assigns it to the current selection of firing points.

fields.name: Exhaustion Script#static script that is run when the task is exhausted
dialogue type#when someone enters this task for the first time, they play this type of dialogue
UI-Controlled
fields.definition: You don't need to modify these here. They are managed by the objectives ui

fields.name: lifetime count#task will never want to suck in more then n guys over lifetime (soft ceiling only applied when limit exceeded
max body count#task becomes inactive after the given number of casualties
min strength:[0,1]#task becomes inactive after the strength of the participants falls below the given level
normal diff count#initial number of actors on normal difficulty
Actor defaults
fields.definition: The following default values are used for spawned actors

fields.name: delay:seconds#how long the AI should pause at this point
angle:degrees#the angle-from-forward that the AI can pick at this point
swarm count#number of cretures in swarm if a swarm is spawned at this location
initial movement distance#before doing anything else, the actor will travel the given distance in its forward direction
parent object#if an object with this name exists, we attach to it as a child
Multiplayer Data
fields.definition: object data for multiplayer game use

fields.name: Player Respawn Weight
fields.definition: This is valid only for objects which are used as player respawn locations

fields.name: dialogue type#when this ending is triggered, launch a dialogue event of the given type
dialogue type#when this ending is triggered, launch a dialogue event of the given type
ecology margin:wus#distance from ecology boundary that creature begins to be repulsed
production frequency bounds:boids/sec#How frequently boids are produced at one of the sources (limited by the max boid count)
source scale to 0 radius:wus#Distance from a source at which the creature scales to full size
sink scale to 0 radius:wus#Distance from a sink at which the creature begins to scale to zero
weight#probability of producing at this source

options: ray trace adjacent bsps on sky hits
lightmaps final gather ignores backfacing hits
lightmaps use more samples for large sky lights
lightmaps use more precise extended gathering (incredibly slow)
not a normally playable space in an MP map (check this on shared BSPs)
If ALL players in trigger volume
Player shot more than X seconds ago
